title: claude-kit project config (v2)
description: Identity layer of a kit project. Resolved in cascade workspace -> project -> island by scripts/lib/kit-config-resolve.sh; the nearest layer wins. The $schema reference in kit.config.json points here so editors validate + autocomplete.

kitVersion.description: Kit version that last wrote/wired this config. Drives self-heal: when it differs from the installed plugin, kit-wire re-converges.

mode.description: How strict the kit is HERE. draft = guardrails off (explore freely); guided = warnings, never block; enforced = guardrails block (worktree-only, PR-per-issue). Set per cascade level — an island can be enforced while its parent workspace is guided.

workspace.description: Present only in kit.workspace.json (the workspace-root marker). Holds settings shared across every project under the workspace.

commons.description: Path (relative to the workspace root) of the promoted skills/agents commons. Default .claude/.

language.description: Comms language, e.g. en, es, mixed.

profile.description: Active role profile (minimal, software, content, research, automation, ...).

skillPrefix.description: Namespace prepended to kit-namespaced workflow skills on scaffold/upgrade (e.g. "kit-" -> kit-task-close). Empty = bare names. See templates/skills/NAMESPACED.

modules.description: Stackable modules added on top of the profile (e.g. "software"). Empty/absent = base project.

baseBranch.description: Integration branch the lifecycle skills branch from and merge into (default: main; e.g. develop).

routines.description: Opt-in scheduled routines accepted by the user (briefing, board sweep, gc, ...). The kit SUGGESTS; the user accepts; nothing is created without opt-in.

upgrade.description: Tells `init.sh --upgrade` which scaffolded paths the project deleted/renamed ON PURPOSE so they are never re-added (#334). Paths are repo-relative (e.g. ".claude/skills/task-new"); a directory entry covers everything beneath it.

removed.description: Scaffolded paths the project deleted on purpose — the upgrade will not re-create them.

renamed.description: Map of original scaffolded path -> the project's new name. The original (key) is treated as removed (never re-added); the value documents where it went.
